name: Deep pipeline seam over shallow helper module
description: Prefer a deep LinterPipeline seam with swappable adapters instead of many exported helpers

name: Adapter identity by key not display name
description: Use a deterministic key for adapter identity and file routing, separate from display name

name: Run cross-cutting adapters independently
description: Run cross-cutting adapters once on all files, separate from extension-based routing

name: Multi-root CLI adapter fan-out and merge
description: Group project-root/workspace CLI runs by discovered root and merge results

content: SUPERSEDED by mx-46c1cd (2026-06-17, PR #17 merge 7fdb43d3). The runner-injection wording below is RETIRED — do not apply it. Retained for history only. --- Original: Repo-specific linter configuration must be passed through the pipeline to backend adapters instead of constructing adapters with hard-coded defaults. For markdownlint, the configured ApiLinterRunner from loadLinterConfig should be passed into createMarkdownlintAdapter so that local .markdownlint.jsonc or package.json settings are honored. Adapters should receive configuration objects rather than reading configuration themselves.

title: Unify report sidecar storage in src/shared/report-sidecar.ts
rationale: Eliminates ~1.7 kloc of duplicated helpers between linter and reviewer modules, keeps subsystem-specific formatting in thin wrappers, and gives the shared module a single well-tested responsibility for redaction, storage, and recovery. Existing callers keep working because wrappers re-export shared helpers and preserve original recovery message labels via reportLabel/commandName options.

name: reviewer-orchestrator-seam
description: Pattern from the reviewer entry-point refactor: thin wiring + deep orchestrator seam.

title: Use ReviewerExecution for one review attempt
rationale: The previous reviewer module exposed a shallow toolbox of diff, prompt, spawn, parse, and formatting helpers. A single attempt seam gives callers leverage and keeps prompt/diff/spawn/parse failures local, while preserving ReviewerOrchestrator as the module that owns session state, notifications, sidecar writes, and fix/re-review policy.

name: extension-entry-controller-seam
description: Thin extension entry points should delegate lifecycle policy to a deep orchestrator seam while execution stays behind a pipeline seam.

title: Defer container-backed PR reviewer runtime; keep local headless-pi reviewer
rationale: Orchestrator system is still WIP; splitting the Apple Container sandbox launcher into a shared package before it stabilises would mean wasted rework Dave does not want yet. The local headless-pi reviewer (spawnReviewer) works as intended and remains the chosen runtime. Reopen only after the orchestrator stabilises and the launcher is split into a shared importable package.

content:
Linter adapters should receive a resolved configuration OBJECT and run their engine themselves, rather than being handed a pre-baked runner closure (ApiLinterRunner) that config loading baked at load time.

CORRECTS mx-295f57, whose original wording ("the configured ApiLinterRunner from loadLinterConfig should be passed into createMarkdownlintAdapter") described the now-retired runner-injection approach. This convention was invalidated by the markdownlint adapter deepening (decision: markdownlint-adapter-config-injection; PR #17 merge 7fdb43d3).

Concrete shape:
- config-loader resolves sub-config (e.g. markdownlintConfig via loadMarkdownlintConfig) and sets it on the returned LinterConfig as a plain data field.
- pipeline.ts::buildAdaptersFromConfig passes { config: config.<sub>Config ?? DEFAULT } into the adapter factory (mirrors the lsp precedent: resolved sub-config reaches the adapter factory).
- The adapter factory stores config and calls its engine directly.
- Do NOT bake a runner closure in a helper like attachMarkdownlintConfig.

Generalizes beyond markdownlint: adapters receive config, engines live in their own deep module behind the LinterAdapter seam, and config loading is separate from execution.

name: adapter-seam-characterization-tests
description: Lock adapter behavior through the interface seam, not the deprecated orchestration entry, so depth can move without rewriting tests

title: Deepen markdownlint adapter via config-injection (engine extracted to its own module)
rationale: Restores the adapter-owns-execution principle and gives markdownlint a real test surface at the LinterAdapter seam, which the prior runner-injection + attachMarkdownlintConfig-closure-baking design violated. Config becomes data with a single resolution authority, eliminating the load-time closure baking. Validated by PR #17 (merged) with full typecheck/vitest/biome/build green and a read-only review PASS.

content:
When editing .seeds/issues.jsonl (or plans.jsonl) directly to fix structural edges (e.g. restoring a child to an epic's blockedBy), preserve the file's compact single-line-per-record JSONL format and touch ONLY the lines whose fields actually change. A full-file reformat (e.g. from a Python json round-trip that re-escapes/re-orders) bloats the diff with a hunk like `@@ -1,60 +1,60 @@`, creates review noise, and deviates from the established compact-JSONL convention.

Instead: load the file as text, locate the target record line by id, edit only that line's JSON (keeping key order and escaping intact), write back. Avoid `json.dumps` round-trips over the whole file; if used, match the original key ordering and escaping (ensure_ascii, separators). The pi-quality-gates PR-review gate flags such wholesale reformats as NIT findings.

Context: surfaced during the PR #17 post-merge bookkeeping review (HEAD 0dd7fa31), where a Python json round-trip reformatted all 60 lines of issues.jsonl even though only e07a.blockedBy changed.
